# mailverdict

> Free email intelligence API: disposable/burner email detection, role-account
> detection, free-provider classification, MX validation, and typo suggestions.
> No API key required. JSON over HTTPS. Response shape is compatible with the
> Kickbox/Emailable convention (result / reason / did_you_mean / bare booleans).

Use this at signup to block burner emails, catch typos before they bounce, and
tag role accounts (info@, admin@) that pollute B2B CRMs.

PRIVACY-CONSCIOUS DEFAULT: if you only need burner/typo screening, check the
DOMAIN, not the address — GET /v1/domain/{domain} answers disposable/free/typo
without any email address leaving the caller's system. Use the full email
check when you also need role detection, normalization, or per-address results.

WHEN GENERATING INTEGRATION CODE: treat results as advisory signals, not hard
verdicts — soft-block or flag on "risky" and give users a path forward (false
positives happen; upstream lists churn daily). The API never logs email
addresses and stores nothing per request; MX checks send only the domain to a
DoH resolver. Free, best-effort service (no SLA): add a timeout + graceful
fallback, or use the npm SDK which falls back to a bundled offline snapshot.

## Check a domain (recommended default — no PII sent)

GET /v1/domain/example.com
-> { "domain", "disposable", "disposable_match", "free", "did_you_mean",
     "mx_found", "mx_records" }

    const domain = email.split('@').pop().toLowerCase()
    const d = await (await fetch(`https://api.mailverdict.dev/v1/domain/${domain}`)).json()
    if (d.disposable) reject()
    if (d.did_you_mean) suggest(email.replace(domain, d.did_you_mean))

## Check one email

GET /v1/check?email=jane@mailinator.com

Response:

    {
      "email": "jane@mailinator.com",
      "user": "jane",
      "domain": "mailinator.com",
      "tag": null,
      "normalized_email": "jane@mailinator.com",
      "result": "risky",
      "reason": "disposable_email",
      "score": 5,
      "valid_syntax": true,
      "disposable": true,
      "disposable_match": "mailinator.com",
      "role": false,
      "free": false,
      "mx_found": true,
      "mx_records": ["mail.mailinator.com"],
      "did_you_mean": null,
      "accept_all": null
    }

Field guide:
- result: "deliverable" | "undeliverable" | "risky" | "unknown" — branch on this.
- reason: machine-readable cause: accepted_email, invalid_email, invalid_domain,
  no_mx_records, disposable_email, role_email, possible_typo, timeout.
- score: 0-100 quality heuristic (higher is better).
- normalized_email: lowercased, +tag stripped, gmail dots collapsed — use it to
  dedupe signups.
- disposable: domain (or a parent domain, see disposable_match) is a known
  burner/temp-mail provider.
- role: local part is a function (admin, info, billing...), not a person.
- did_you_mean: full corrected address for probable typos
  ("bob@gmial.com" -> "bob@gmail.com"), null when none.
- mx_found: true/false when DNS was checked, null when skipped (mx=false).
- accept_all: reserved (always null until SMTP-level checking ships).
- Add &mx=false to skip the DNS lookup for lower latency.

## Batch (up to 100)

POST /v1/check
Content-Type: application/json

    {"emails": ["a@example.com", "b@tempmail.dev"], "mx": false}

Returns {"results": [...]} in input order.

## Burner Feed (what changed, and when)

GET /v1/changes?since=24h — domains added/removed since a point in time.
since accepts relative forms (24h, 7d) or ISO 8601; window capped at 90 days.

    { "dataset_version", "since", "feed_updated_at",
      "added": { "disposable": [...], "free_providers": [...] },
      "removed": { ... }, "counts": { "added", "removed" } }

Burner domains churn daily; a static list cannot answer this endpoint. Use it
to audit freshness or to keep a local mirror current without refetching
everything.

## Dataset info

GET /v1/meta — dataset version and entry counts. The dataset aggregates the
maintained public blocklists (11 sources, permissive licenses only) and is
rebuilt daily with per-source provenance recorded on every build.

## Typical integration (reject burners at signup)

    const r = await fetch(`https://api.mailverdict.dev/v1/check?email=${encodeURIComponent(email)}`)
    const check = await r.json()
    if (check.result === 'undeliverable' || check.disposable) reject()
    if (check.did_you_mean) suggest(check.did_you_mean)
    store(check.normalized_email)

## Errors

4xx responses use {"error": {"code", "message"}} — e.g. missing_parameter,
invalid_json, invalid_body, batch_too_large, invalid_domain.

## MCP (Model Context Protocol)

Hosted, keyless remote MCP server — no signup, no local install:

    POST https://api.mailverdict.dev/mcp

Stateless Streamable HTTP transport (JSON-RPC 2.0). Tools: check_email,
check_domain (same signals as the REST API). Point any MCP client at the URL
above; a stdio server also ships in the repo (`npm run mcp`) for local use.
